mapping.a_element: transonic MHD accretion flow around the compact object (background flow)
mapping.b_element: steady radial shallow-water flow that forms the circular hydraulic jump (background flow)

mapping.a_element: sonic/critical point of the accretion flow where flow speed equals the signal speed
mapping.b_element: critical point where the flow speed equals the shallow-water surface-wave speed, i.e. the acoustic white-hole horizon of the emulated metric
super-critical region of the shallow-water flow
sub-critical region of the shallow-water flow

mapping.a_element: MHD shock wave forming in the flow
conserved energy and angular momentum parameterizing the solution topologies
mapping.b_element: flow parameters (Froude number / flow rate) selecting the jump behavior

brief_justification: Both are transonic critical-flow problems in which the fluid passes through a sonic/critical point separating a super-critical from a sub-critical region and forms a shock: the MHD shock in the accretion flow plays the same role as the shallow-water hydraulic jump, and the accretion sonic point corresponds to the critical point that paper B explicitly identifies as an acoustic white-hole horizon. This is the standard Froude<->Mach hydraulic/analogue-gravity correspondence, so the same critical-flow machinery drives both.
